calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Request To Enter Default And Vacate Default Judgement
Off Calendar Jan-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Kaufhold, Esq. 415-944-9402 of Kaufhold Gaskin LLP for Plaintiff.
Jon O' Donnell, Esq. 415-722-5527 for Defendant Ira Schanzer.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's thoughts. Plaintiff argues CCP 415.5. and no alternative service(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT IRA SCHANZER's Motion To Set Aside Request To Enter Default And Vacate Default Judgement.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendant Ira Schanzer's motion to set aside the August 19, 2014 default and the March 24, 2015 default judgment entered against him is granted. Due process demands, and the doctrine of extrinsic mistake permits, the vacation of the default and default judgment based on the undisputed fact that Mr. Schanzer had no knowledge of this case until November 2018 and that service by publication occurred without plaintiff Zakaria Saidin exhausting all possible sources to be able to serve Mr. Schanzer personally or by substitute service. Mr. Schanzer has shown that due to his "excusable neglect" of not receiving notice of this case by the service by publication he failed to timely appear to prevent the entry of his default and default judgment. Mr. Schanzer has submitted more than ample evidence to satisfy the three part test to vacate a default and default judgment due to extrinsic mistake. (Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2007) 146 Cal. App. 4th 488, 503). Mr. Schanzer has shown that he has a meritorious case by the affirmative defense of self-defense. Mr. Schanzer has shown a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense prior to the entry of his default by his lack of knowledge of this case. Mr. Schanzer filed this motion very promptly after learning that his default and default judgment had been entered. Any money or property received by Mr. Saidin or his counsel or anyone on their behalf in collecting on the judgment must be returned to Mr. Schanzer through his counsel immediately. Mr. Schanzer must file his answer no later than January 29, 2019.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
